name: Review design package and pilot gate
datum: Top face A, left end B, bar centerline C
checks: C3/A4/C6 pilot bars identified; actual wood species and tube bore confirmed
notes: Do not cut full set until pilot bars validate K constant and arch schedule.

name: Surface and thickness bar blanks
tool: Jointer knives, planer, drum sander, calipers
datum: Top face A and grain arrow
checks: Thickness 0.875 in +/- 0.005; grain direction logged; defects outside finished area
notes: Leave length and width allowance for tabs and final tuning cleanup.

name: Label blanks and mark nodes
tool: Marking knife, square, center punch, calipers
datum: Left end B and centerline C
outputs: Labeled blanks with note, target Hz, nodes
checks: node_1 and node_2 match family-spec.csv; top face remains marked
notes: Cord/support holes belong at free-free nodes.

name: Prepare CNC fixture and datum verification
tool: Spoilboard surfacing bit, edge finder/probe, clamps/tape
workholding: Replaceable spoilboard, datum fence, optional vacuum plus clamps
datum: Machine X0 at left end B, Y0 at centerline C, Z0 top face A
checks: Spoilboard surfaced; clamps clear toolpath; sample air-cut passes
notes: This operation creates confidence before Padauk is loaded.

name: Rough profile bars with tabs
tool: 1/4 in downcut spiral; optional 1/8 in upcut for small reliefs
workholding: Spoilboard tape/clamps, tabs at non-vibrating ends
datum: Top face A, left end B, centerline C
checks: Tabs do not overlap node holes or arch region; final length retains tuning allowance
notes: Use conservative depth per pass and preserve labeled orientation.

name: 3D arch underside relief
tool: 3/4 in ball-end mill; 1/2 in ball-end for treble alternative
workholding: Cradle or flip fixture supporting top face without denting
datum: Top face A re-referenced after flip; left end B retained
outputs: Underside arch cut on each bar
checks: Center thickness never below 0.250 in; arch centered; no chatter/burn
notes: Use parabolic toolpath over about 60 percent of bar length. This is the pitch-lowering operation.

name: Drill support holes at nodes
tool: 1/4 in brad-point bit or boring tool
checks: Hole breakout absent; node positions within +/- 0.020 in; holes deburred
notes: Pilot on scrap before drilling tuned bars.

name: Hand tune bars
tool: Soft node supports, tuner, sanding block, scraper, scale
workholding: Padded bench, supports at node_1 and node_2
outputs: Measured pitch log and tuned bars
checks: Measured Hz recorded at flat/post-arch/post-sand/final; cents error computed
notes: Remove underside center material to lower pitch. Sneak up slowly.

name: Cut and deburr resonator tubes
outputs: Oversize resonator tubes labeled by note
checks: Tube ID matches updated bore; cut length includes trim allowance; burrs removed
notes: Recalculate lengths if selected bore differs from current proxy.

name: Cap and mount resonators
datum: Tube open end and bar centerline
checks: Caps seal without buzz; tubes clear bars/rails; note labels visible
notes: Use removable caps for first validation build.

name: Build frame and support rails
tool: Saw blade, drill bits, router bits
checks: Supports land at nodes; cross members removable; mallet and resonator clearance checked
notes: Temporary validation frame is acceptable before final furniture-grade frame.

name: Final validation and release
datum: Same support and strike datum used for every note
checks: All notes measured; outliers flagged; resonator response notes captured
notes: Do not treat dimensions as final until measured data closes the loop.
